full_description: Count the green target blocks on the table, then press the number button matching the count; from left to right the buttons represent 1, 2, and 3.
schema: {A} denotes the green target blocks, {B} denotes the matching number button, {a} denotes the arm used to press the button, {x} denotes the number of green target blocks
preference: Mention counting target blocks, the left-to-right 1/2/3 button mapping, and pressing the matching button once. Do not reveal the counted answer in subtask instructions.
seen: Count {A}, then press the matching button with {a}; the buttons are 1, 2, 3 from left to right.
Look around, count the green blocks, and use the left-to-right 1/2/3 buttons to answer.
Find how many {A} are present, then press the matching number button.
Use vision to count {A}; then use {a} to press the matching button.
Count the target blocks first, then confirm with the button whose left-to-right position matches the count.
unseen: Count the green blocks and press the matching button, where left to right means 1, 2, and 3.
After counting {A}, press the corresponding button once.
Use {a} to push the button whose left-to-right order equals the green-block count.
look around to count the green target blocks

seen: press the button matching the counted result with {a}
